title: Cut-completed boostless scalar-exchange trispectrum
one_sentence_idea: Use the bulk resonant cutting rule of arXiv:2112.03448 to compute the nonanalytic collapsed-channel signal in the scalar trispectrum from heavy-scalar exchange, then extend it into a full four-point template up to controlled contact ambiguities.
motivation: The seed paper gives a practical way to isolate cosmological-collider signals as resonant production/decay events, but the domain summary suggests exchange trispectra are less systematically reconstructed than contact trispectra. A focused target is the connected four-point function of curvature perturbations, <zeta zeta zeta zeta>, generated by a massive scalar sigma exchanged between two boost-breaking cubic vertices such as sigma dot{pi}^2. This sits between existing contact-trispectrum reconstruction and bispectrum collider bootstrap work.
calculation_target: Compute the s-channel collapsed limit q = |k1+k2| << k_i of the tree-level scalar trispectrum for L_int = a^3 lambda sigma dot{pi}^2, with m_sigma/H > 3/2 and optionally c_sigma != 1. The first deliverable is the coefficient and phase of the oscillatory nonanalytic terms of schematic form T_zeta^(s) ~ C_+(r_L,r_R,c_sigma,mu) (q/K_L)^(3/2+i mu)(q/K_R)^(3/2-i mu) + c.c., where mu = sqrt(m_sigma^2/H^2 - 9/4), K_L=k1+k2, K_R=k3+k4, and r_L=k1/k2, r_R=k3/k4.
method_sketch: Start from the in-in exchange integral but do not evaluate the full double time integral directly. Apply the arXiv:2112.03448 resonant-cut prescription to replace the massive internal line by its on-shell Wightman/cut contribution, reducing the leading collider signal to a product of two time integrals at the left and right cubic vertices. Then compare this discontinuity with single-cut/dispersion formulas: use the cut result as input data, reconstruct the nonlocal part of the four-point function, and classify the remaining ambiguity using the known boostless contact-trispectrum basis. Keep the first calculation narrow: one interaction, one channel, general external pair ratios, then add permutations only after the s-channel signal is under control.
first_checks: Recover the known de Sitter-invariant or equal-sound-speed scalar-exchange result in the appropriate limit, using arXiv:1811.00024 as the benchmark.
Verify that the result has the expected pair-exchange symmetry k1 <-> k2 and k3 <-> k4, then add the t/u channel permutations.
Check that the discontinuity agrees with the single-cut logic of arXiv:2104.06587 and the more recent dispersion reconstruction picture of arXiv:2602.05546.
Separate nonanalytic collider terms from analytic/contact terms; confirm that analytic pieces can be absorbed into the contact basis of arXiv:2106.15468.

As a phenomenology check, project the resulting shape onto the separable scalar-field-exchange templates used in arXiv:2502.04434 and identify whether the boost-breaking phase or pair-ratio dependence is actually distinguishable.
literature_checks: ARC paper MCP tools were attempted first for metadata/abstract/TOC checks, but the calls returned as cancelled, so I used arXiv pages for source checks.
arXiv:2112.03448 confirms the relevant seed idea: cosmological collider signals are classified into local/nonlocal pieces and extracted by a practical cutting rule: https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.03448

arXiv:1811.00024 already solves important scalar-exchange four-point functions using conformal symmetry, singularities, spin raising, and weight shifting, so novelty should avoid merely recomputing that case: https://arxiv.org/abs/1811.00024
arXiv:2106.15468 covers the all-derivative contact scalar trispectrum from amplitudes, making contact ambiguities a known component rather than the main target: https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.15468

arXiv:2104.06587 gives single-cut rules and explicitly includes four massless scalars exchanging a massive scalar as a check, so the proposed calculation should emphasize boost-breaking/c_sigma dependence and template construction: https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.06587
arXiv:2205.00013 builds the boostless collider bootstrap mainly for bispectra, motivating the analogous boostless exchange-trispectrum calculation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.00013

arXiv:2602.05546 and arXiv:2602.03841 indicate a fresh 2026 direction: reconstructing correlators from discontinuities/dressing rules, which can turn the cut signal into more than a collapsed-limit estimate: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.05546 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.03841
key_papers: arXiv:2112.03448, Tong-Wang-Zhu, Cutting Rule for Cosmological Collider Signals: A Bulk Evolution Perspective.
arXiv:1811.00024, Arkani-Hamed-Baumann-Lee-Pimentel, The Cosmological Bootstrap: Inflationary Correlators from Symmetries and Singularities.
arXiv:2106.15468, Bonifacio-Pajer-Wang, From Amplitudes to Contact Cosmological Correlators.
arXiv:2205.00013, Pimentel-Wang, Boostless Cosmological Collider Bootstrap.
arXiv:2502.04434, Philcox, Searching for Inflationary Physics with the CMB Trispectrum: Primordial Theory and Optimal Estimators.
arXiv:2602.05546, Das-Karan-Khatun-Kundu, Reconstructing cosmological correlators via dispersion: from cutting to dressing rules.
novelty_uncertainty: The fully de Sitter-invariant scalar-exchange trispectrum and general cutting rules are not new. The potentially publishable gap is a clean, explicit boost-breaking scalar-exchange trispectrum, with its collider phase and pair-ratio dependence derived by the 2112.03448 bulk-resonance cut and organized so it can feed modern CMB trispectrum templates. I am uncertain whether a very similar sigma dot{pi}^2 four-point result exists in older quasi-single-field trispectrum papers; that should be checked before committing. If it exists, switch the first model to c_sigma != 1 or to a small basis of independent boost-breaking cubic vertices.
response_to_reviewer: If a reviewer says this is only a rederivation of known scalar exchange, narrow the claim: the calculation is not the existence of the collapsed oscillation, but the cut-based derivation of its coefficient and phase for boost-breaking interactions, plus a dispersion/contact decomposition that produces a template-ready trispectrum with controlled analytic ambiguities.
